title: raoulia hectorii var. mollis
intro: Raoulia hectorii var. mollis, also known as the 'Pill Millie', is a succulent plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its silvery-green leaves that resemble a ball of cotton. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Raoulia hectorii var. mollis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource for holistic health practitioners.
